name: Angrboda
description: Angrboða, whose name translates to "she who offers sorrow" or "sorrow-bringer," is a jötunn (giantess) in Norse mythology. She is primarily known for her association with Loki and for being the mother of three monstrous offspring: the wolf Fenrir, the serpent Jörmungandr, and the ruler of Hel, Hel. Angrboða's role is significant as she is a progenitor of beings that directly contribute to the events of Ragnarök, the end of the world in Norse cosmology. Her existence highlights the anxieties surrounding chaos and the untamed aspects of nature present within the Norse worldview. While her individual actions are not extensively detailed in surviving texts, her impact on the mythological narrative is profound due to the catastrophic potential of her children, who represent existential threats to the gods and the established order of the cosmos. Her dwelling in Jötunheimr, the land of the giants, further emphasizes her connection to the primordial forces that stand in opposition to the Aesir gods.
subtitle: Sorrow-Bringer

keyMyths.title: The Binding of Fenrir
keyMyths.description: Angrboða's son, Fenrir, grew to an immense size, alarming the gods. They attempted to bind him with increasingly strong chains, but he broke free each time. Finally, the dwarves crafted Gleipnir, a seemingly soft and silken rope made from impossible components. Fenrir suspected treachery but agreed to be bound if one of the gods placed their hand in his mouth as a guarantee. Tyr volunteered, knowing he would lose his hand, and Fenrir was successfully bound, awaiting Ragnarök. This myth highlights the fear and inevitability surrounding Angrboða's offspring.

keyMyths.title: Jörmungandr in the Ocean
keyMyths.description: Another of Angrboða's children, Jörmungandr, the Midgard Serpent, was cast into the ocean surrounding Midgard by Odin. The serpent grew to such enormous proportions that it encircled the entire world, biting its own tail. During Ragnarök, Jörmungandr will rise from the sea, poisoning the land and sky with its venom, and engage in a deadly battle with Thor, ultimately resulting in the mutual destruction of both combatants. This emphasizes the destructive potential of the giantess's offspring.

keyMyths.title: Hel's Dominion
keyMyths.description: Angrboða's daughter, Hel, was appointed by Odin to rule over Helheim, the realm of the dead. She governs those who die of sickness or old age, creating a distinct afterlife realm separate from Valhalla. Hel is depicted as half-living and half-corpse, reflecting the duality of life and death. During Ragnarök, Hel will lead an army of the dead against the gods, further illustrating the catastrophic consequences stemming from Angrboða's lineage and the threats of the underworld.
